content.problemStatement:
Alice has a rectangular bar of chocolate of dimensions N×MN\times MN×M. The bar is made up of N⋅MN\cdot MN⋅M smaller 1×11\times 11×1 squares.

Unfortunately, Alice has to share part of the chocolate with Bob.

**At most once**, Alice can break the rectangular bar into two smaller rectangles, either by making a vertical cut or a horizontal cut. She'll then give one of the pieces to Bob, and keep the other for herself.

The two smaller rectangles must both have **integer** dimensions.

Note that Alice can also choose to not break the bar at all, if she wishes to.

Bob won't be happy unless he receives **at least** KKK of the 1×11\times 11×1 squares of chocolate.

What's the largest number of squares of chocolate Alice can keep with her, while ensuring that Bob is happy with what he receives?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of one line containing three space-separated integers N,M,N, M,N,M, and KKK – the dimensions of the chocolate bar, and the number of squares Bob wants.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum number of 1×11\times 11×1 chocolate that Alice can keep with her.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The chocolate bar is of dimensions 3×43\times 43×4, and Bob wants at least 222 squares.
Alice can break the bar into 3×13\times 13×1 and 3×33\times 33×3 rectangles, and give the smaller one (which has three squares) to Bob. This lets her keep 3⋅3=93\cdot 3 = 93⋅3=9 squares.
Test case 222: The chocolate bar is again of dimensions 3×43\times 43×4, but this time Bob wants at least 444 squares.
Now, the best Alice can do is to break the bar into 1×41\times 41×4 and 2×42\times 42×4, and give Bob the 1×41\times 41×4 piece. This lets her keep 2⋅4=82\cdot 4 = 82⋅4=8 squares.
Test case 333: The chocolate bar is of dimensions 6×56\times 56×5, but this time Bob wants at least 282828 squares.
If Alice cuts the bar at all, both pieces will have less than 282828 pieces, so neither piece can be given to Bob.
So, Alice's only option is to give the entire bar to Bob without making a cut, and so she'll have no chocolate left.
Test case 555: Bob wants at least 000 pieces, so Alice can just keep the entire 5×55\times 55×5 bar with her without cutting it.
